title: Set write concern to match durability needs
description: Use w:majority for data that must survive a primary failover, accepting the latency it adds.
body: Use when deciding how durable a write must be on a replica set. Do not leave durable writes at w:1, which acknowledges as soon as the primary has it; if that primary fails before replication, the write is rolled back and lost. Actions: set writeConcern w:majority for important data so acknowledgement waits for a majority of voting members, pair it with readConcern majority for read-your-write consistency, and verify with a failover test. Validation signals include the write surviving a stepDown of the primary and the rollback log staying empty. Risk boundary: w:majority adds latency proportional to the slowest majority member, so reserve w:1 for truly disposable data and document the tradeoff.
